scenarios.avoid_when: The task is about a different RL library, general RL theory without CleanRL code, or executing long benchmark/cloud jobs without CleanRL-specific context.
scenarios.best_for: Choosing and safely running CleanRL algorithms, inspecting script arguments, diagnosing missing RL backends, and adapting single-file training commands.
scenarios.read_when: cleanrl, CleanRL, ppo.py, dqn.py, c51.py, Atari, EnvPool, Procgen, MuJoCo, PettingZoo, JAX, tyro, --total-timesteps, --env-id
scenarios.role: Routes CleanRL script-oriented RL training tasks to algorithm, CLI, optional dependency, and safe-run guidance.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Prefer `cleanrl` over generic Python/ML guidance when the user names CleanRL or CleanRL script files; use training-scripts first for algorithm commands.
scenarios.title: Reinforcement Learning Training Scripts
scenarios.when_to_read: Use for CleanRL training commands, RL algorithm script selection, tiny smoke runs, optional backend extras, or single-file script adaptation.

scenarios.avoid_when: The task is only about selecting one training script or evaluating a saved model artifact; route those to the training or evaluation sub-skills.
scenarios.best_for: Generating dry-run command matrices, checking credential readiness without leaking secrets, and planning cloud/container/Slurm operations safely.
scenarios.read_when: cleanrl benchmark, cleanrl_utils.benchmark, wandb cleanRL, Optuna tuner, resume training, reproduce, Slurm, AWS Batch, Docker, Terraform, benchmark/*.sh
scenarios.role: Provides side-effect-aware operations guidance for CleanRL experiment orchestration and infrastructure integrations.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose this scenario when the user asks how to run many experiments, track or reproduce results, or use CleanRL cloud/benchmark tooling.
scenarios.title: RL Experiment Operations
scenarios.when_to_read: Use for CleanRL W&B tracking, benchmark command matrices, tuning, plotting, resume/reproduce, Docker, Slurm, Terraform, or AWS Batch planning.

scenarios.avoid_when: The user only wants to run an existing CleanRL command without changing the repository.
scenarios.best_for: Selecting focused native checks, preserving CleanRL style, updating docs and tests after script changes, and planning performance regression validation.
scenarios.read_when: modify cleanrl script, add CLI flag, update docs/rl-algorithms, tests/test_*.py, pyproject.toml, requirements, CONTRIBUTING, RLOps regression
scenarios.role: Keeps CleanRL changes aligned with single-file implementation conventions, docs/tests, optional extras, and focused validation.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Use this scenario for maintainer-style code and documentation tasks; cross-route to training-scripts for flag semantics and experiment-operations for benchmarks.
scenarios.title: Repo Maintenance and Contribution
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when editing CleanRL code, docs, tests, package metadata, requirements, or contribution/RLOps workflows.
